title: 1.1: Tests that rewrapping from a populated source buffer into a destination buffer with identical width and height preserves line content exactly (for every row i, destination line i equals source line i after rewrap)

1.2: Tests that rewrapping into a wider destination buffer (destination width > source width) merges continued source lines into a single logical line by asserting destination line content includes text that originally spanned a continued boundary in the source

1.3: Tests that rewrapping into a narrower destination buffer (destination width < source width) splits a long logical line across multiple destination rows by asserting the expected per-row destination line content after rewrap
1.4: Includes at least one test that captures the cursor position returned by the rewrap operation and asserts exact equality against an expected value (not just non-None/truthiness)
1.7: Narrower-destination rewrap tests explicitly assert that the first destination row is not a continuation line (i.e., lb2.is_continued(0) is asserted False after rewrap)
1.8: Narrower-destination rewrap tests assert that wrapped output sets continuation flags on at least one destination row with index > 0 (i.e., lb2.is_continued(i) asserted True for some i > 0)

1.9: Wider-destination rewrap tests include at least one explicit assertion that two adjacent destination rows have different line text values after rewrap (i.e., they are not merged into a single identical line string)
2.1: Places the test for rewrapping with identical buffer dimensions in kitty_tests/datatypes.py
2.2: Places the test for rewrapping to wider destination buffer in kitty_tests/datatypes.py
2.3: Places the test for rewrapping to narrower destination buffer in kitty_tests/datatypes.py
3.1: All tests use Python unittest framework by inheriting from BaseTest class, using assertion methods like self.ae() for equality checks
3.2: All tests follow the test naming pattern test_rewrap_<scenario> where scenario describes the rewrap condition being tested
4.1: Wider-buffer rewrap tests include an explicit assertion over destination continuation flags (e.g., checks lb2.is_continued(i) equals expected booleans for at least two relevant rows)
4.2: Wider-buffer rewrap tests include an explicit assertion that destination dirty line tracking (lb2.dirty_lines()) equals the expected list of dirty row indices after rewrap

4.3: Narrower-buffer rewrap tests include an explicit assertion over destination continuation flags indicating wrapped content (i.e., at least one destination row produced by wrapping is asserted to be continued)

4.4: Narrower-buffer rewrap tests include a case where the source logical line contains trailing spaces at a wrap boundary and asserts those spaces are preserved in the destination output (not trimmed/dropped)
